title: Your First Gateway
description: Start the OpenClaw Gateway, understand what it does, and verify it's running correctly.
content_mdx:
# Your First Gateway

The Gateway is the heart of OpenClaw. It's a process that runs on your machine and handles everything: routing messages, calling AI, managing memory, and more.

Let's start it.

## Starting the Gateway

```bash
openclaw gateway start
```

You'll see output like:

```
🦞 OpenClaw Gateway v2.x.x starting...
✅ Config loaded
✅ AI provider connected (claude-3-5-sonnet)
✅ Gateway listening on http://localhost:3001
🚀 Gateway ready!
```

If you see all green checkmarks, your gateway is running!

## What the Gateway Does

When the Gateway is running, it:

- **Listens for messages** from connected channels (Telegram, Discord, etc.)
- **Processes requests** through your configured AI model
- **Manages sessions** — keeps track of conversations
- **Executes skills** — runs code and tools on your behalf
- **Handles memory** — reads/writes MEMORY.md and daily notes
- **Exposes a control UI** — a web dashboard at http://localhost:3001

## The Control UI

Open your browser and go to:

```
http://localhost:3001
```

You'll see the OpenClaw dashboard with:

- Gateway status
- Active sessions
- Connected channels
- Message logs
- Settings

## Checking Gateway Status

```bash
openclaw gateway status
```

This shows you if the gateway is running, what channels are connected, and any errors.

## Running in the Background

By default, `openclaw gateway start` runs in the foreground. To run it in the background:

```bash
# macOS/Linux:
openclaw gateway start --background

# Or use a process manager:
npm install -g pm2
pm2 start openclaw -- gateway start
pm2 save
pm2 startup
```

With pm2, your gateway will restart automatically if your computer reboots.

## Stopping the Gateway

```bash
openclaw gateway stop
```

## Restarting After Config Changes

Whenever you change your configuration (channels, models, etc.), restart the gateway:

```bash
openclaw gateway restart
```

## Gateway Configuration File

The gateway configuration lives at `~/.openclaw/config.json`. You shouldn't need to edit this directly — the Control UI handles most settings — but it's good to know where it is.

```json
{
  "model": "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "channels": {},
  "port": 3001
}
```

## Common Issues

**Port 3001 already in use:**
```bash
# Use a different port:
openclaw gateway start --port 3002
```

**API key not working:**
```bash
# Reconfigure your API key:
openclaw config set api_key YOUR_KEY_HERE
```

**Gateway won't start:**
```bash
# Check the logs:
openclaw gateway logs
```

## Congratulations!

You have a running Gateway. In the next module, we'll explore the Control UI and understand everything you can do from the dashboard.

⚡ +50 XP for getting your Gateway online!
